name: zvideo_dd_lock_surface_state
description: zVideo_dd::LockSurfaceState skips the windowed display-mode surface, skips already-locked states, locks an unlocked DirectDraw surface, and copies the lock descriptor into the surface state.
known_limits: VC6 cl 12.00.8168 x86 /O2 /Ob0 /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?LockSurfaceState@zVideo_dd@@YIHPAUzVideo_SurfaceStatePartial@@@Z still fails: the compiler folds the display-mode equality branch into the locked check and emits a different copy/epilogue layout. The reviewed run reported 75 unmasked mismatches, 12 relocation-masked bytes, and no trailing NOP trim.
functional_evidence: Binary Ninja assembly for 0x4a7fc0 returns zero when fullscreen is disabled and the argument is g_zVideo_DisplayModeSurfaceState, returns zero when surfaceState->locked is nonzero, calls zVideo_dd::LockDirectDrawSurface(surfaceState->surf, &outLockedSurfaceDesc), and on success copies dwWidth, dwHeight, lPitch, and lpSurface into the state while setting locked and lockInfoValid to one.
Binary Ninja decompilation for 0x4a7fc0 has no warnings and matches the implemented skip, DirectDraw lock, descriptor-copy, and return-result behavior.

The native skip smoke verifies the windowed display-mode skip and already-locked skip paths. The DirectDraw surface-state smoke verifies the unlocked path calls the fake DirectDraw Lock slot through zVideo_dd::LockDirectDrawSurface, then copies width, height, pitch, pixels, locked, and lockInfoValid from the returned descriptor.
